description_long: This object is a long, slender sword with a gently curved blade, reminiscent of a traditional Japanese katana. The blade appears to be made of polished steel, with a subtle shine and a single edge running the length of the curve. The handle is straight and wrapped, likely with a textured material for grip, and features a small, circular guard separating it from the blade. The guard and handle fittings have a metallic, possibly brass or gold-toned finish, adding a touch of elegance. The sword is accompanied by a matching scabbard or sheath, which covers the blade and is similarly curved. The overall design is both functional and visually striking, suitable for display or ceremonial use as well as practical application.
description: A long, curved sword with a polished steel blade, wrapped handle, and matching curved sheath, featuring metallic accents.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved blade and sheath, with the handle and guard clearly visible.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the circular guard and the alignment of the handle with the sheath.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the curvature of the blade and sheath, with the handle and guard visible from a different angle.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the straight alignment of the handle and the narrow profile of the blade and sheath.
attribute_handle_design: straight, wrapped, with metallic fittings and a circular guard
